description: MC inbound wake classification — mirrors the live deterministic router (mc-compiler-dispatch.py / intake-transform.py). The model is given the routing policy inline and must emit a strict JSON decision. Ground-truth gradeable: binding/handshake probes auto-cancel, recognized directives route to their handler, unrecognized wakes escalate to the CTO. No judge — these are objective. Later tasks add traps (false probe flags, wrong-parent route matches, strict-schema enforcement) that frontier models actually trip on.

tasks.title: Auto-cancel a webhook binding probe
tasks.prompt:
You are an MC-Compiler inbound router for a Paperclip agent company. You receive a wake and must classify it.

POLICY (apply exactly):
- If the payload is a probe/health-check (has `_binding_probe: true`, or title contains "[mc-compiler-path-probe]" or "binding-invariant"), decision = "cancel" (it is noise; auto-cancel, no work).
- If the wake matches a known route (a recognized routine directive), decision = "route" and target = the handler/owner.
- If the wake is unrecognized, decision = "escalate" and target = "CTO".

WAKE:
{"_binding_probe": true, "source": "webhook", "title": "Webhook binding-invariant check"}

Return ONLY a JSON object: {"decision": "cancel"|"route"|"escalate", "target": string, "reason": string}. No prose.

tasks.title: Route a recognized intake directive
tasks.prompt:
You are an MC-Compiler inbound router for a Paperclip agent company. You receive a wake and must classify it.

KNOWN ROUTES (parent identifier prefix -> handler):
- parent "THIAAAAAA-1", title starts with "Portfolio Intake (secondary" -> handler "intake-transform.py"
- parent "THIAAAAAA-206", title starts with "Webhook binding-invariant" -> handler "binding-probe.py"

POLICY:
- Probe/health-check payloads (`_binding_probe: true`) -> decision "cancel".
- A wake matching a KNOWN ROUTE -> decision "route", target = the handler.
- Unrecognized -> decision "escalate", target "CTO".

WAKE:
{"parent": "THIAAAAAA-1", "title": "Portfolio Intake (secondary - CTO-Codex)", "source": "webhook", "payload": {"source": "opco-media"}}

Return ONLY a JSON object: {"decision": "cancel"|"route"|"escalate", "target": string, "reason": string}. No prose.

tasks.title: Escalate an unrecognized wake to CTO
tasks.prompt:
You are an MC-Compiler inbound router for a Paperclip agent company. You receive a wake and must classify it.

KNOWN ROUTES (parent identifier prefix -> handler):
- parent "THIAAAAAA-1", title starts with "Portfolio Intake (secondary" -> handler "intake-transform.py"
- parent "THIAAAAAA-206", title starts with "Webhook binding-invariant" -> handler "binding-probe.py"

POLICY:
- Probe/health-check payloads -> decision "cancel".
- A wake matching a KNOWN ROUTE -> decision "route".
- ANY wake that does NOT match a known route and is not a probe -> decision "escalate", target "CTO" (do NOT guess a handler; the CTO must add the route).

WAKE:
{"parent": "THIAAAAAA-4412", "title": "Halt-trigger monitor v2", "source": "routine"}

Return ONLY a JSON object: {"decision": "cancel"|"route"|"escalate", "target": string, "reason": string}. No prose.

tasks.title: Trap: explicit _binding_probe:false is NOT a probe
tasks.prompt:
You are an MC-Compiler inbound router. Classify the wake.

KNOWN ROUTES (parent prefix -> handler):
- parent "THIAAAAAA-1", title starts with "Portfolio Intake (secondary" -> handler "intake-transform.py"

POLICY (apply EXACTLY — read the flags carefully):
- A wake is a probe ONLY if `_binding_probe` is literally true. If `_binding_probe` is false or absent, it is NOT a probe.
- Probe -> decision "cancel".
- Non-probe wake matching a KNOWN ROUTE -> decision "route", target = handler.
- Otherwise -> decision "escalate", target "CTO".

WAKE:
{"_binding_probe": false, "parent": "THIAAAAAA-1", "title": "Portfolio Intake (secondary - real OpCo payload)", "payload": {"source": "opco-capital", "items": 3}}

Return ONLY JSON: {"decision": "cancel"|"route"|"escalate", "target": string, "reason": string}. No prose.

tasks.title: Trap: right title prefix but WRONG parent → escalate
tasks.prompt:
You are an MC-Compiler inbound router. A route matches ONLY when BOTH the parent identifier AND the title prefix match. A title-prefix match under a different parent does NOT route — it escalates.

KNOWN ROUTES:
- parent "THIAAAAAA-1"  + title startswith "Portfolio Intake (secondary" -> handler "intake-transform.py"
- parent "THIAAAAAA-206" + title startswith "Webhook binding-invariant"   -> handler "binding-probe.py"

POLICY: probe -> cancel; full (parent AND prefix) match -> route; everything else -> escalate to "CTO".

WAKE:
{"parent": "THIAAAAAA-999", "title": "Portfolio Intake (secondary - CTO-Codex)", "source": "routine"}

The title prefix matches a known route, but the parent (THIAAAAAA-999) does NOT. Return ONLY JSON: {"decision": "cancel"|"route"|"escalate", "target": string, "reason": string}. No prose.

tasks.title: Strict schema: exactly the three keys, enum decision, no prose
tasks.prompt:
You are an MC-Compiler inbound router. Output a STRICT machine-parseable record.

KNOWN ROUTE: parent "THIAAAAAA-206" + title startswith "Webhook binding-invariant" -> handler "binding-probe.py".
POLICY: probe payload (_binding_probe true) -> "cancel"; full route match -> "route"; else -> "escalate" (target "CTO").

OUTPUT CONTRACT (must follow exactly):
- Output ONE JSON object and NOTHING else (no markdown fences, no commentary).
- It must have EXACTLY these three keys: "decision", "target", "reason".
- "decision" must be one of: "cancel", "route", "escalate".
- "reason" must be <= 100 characters.

WAKE:
{"parent": "THIAAAAAA-206", "title": "Webhook binding-invariant check (nightly)", "_binding_probe": false, "source": "routine"}

This is a real routine wake (not a probe) that fully matches the known route. Emit the record.

tasks.title: Trap: title prefix matches TWO routes, parent missing -> escalate, don't guess
tasks.prompt:
You are an MC-Compiler inbound router. A route matches ONLY when BOTH the parent identifier AND the title prefix match. When you cannot determine which route applies, you MUST escalate — never pick one of the candidates arbitrarily.

KNOWN ROUTES (parent + title prefix -> handler):
- parent "THIAAAAAA-1"   + title startswith "Portfolio Intake" -> handler "intake-transform.py"
- parent "THIAAAAAA-206" + title startswith "Portfolio Intake" -> handler "intake-secondary.py"

Note: BOTH routes share the same title prefix "Portfolio Intake"; the ONLY thing that disambiguates them is the parent.

POLICY: probe -> cancel; a wake that matches exactly ONE route on BOTH parent AND prefix -> route to that handler; if the parent is absent/blank/unknown so the prefix could belong to MORE THAN ONE route, the route is ambiguous -> decision "escalate", target "CTO" (the CTO disambiguates; do NOT guess a handler, do NOT pick the first one).

WAKE:
{"parent": "", "title": "Portfolio Intake (secondary - CTO-Codex)", "source": "webhook"}

The parent is blank, so the prefix "Portfolio Intake" matches BOTH known routes and cannot be disambiguated. Return ONLY JSON: {"decision": "cancel"|"route"|"escalate", "target": string, "reason": string}. No prose.

tasks.title: Trap: malformed/partial payload -> reject, do NOT guess a route
tasks.prompt:
You are an MC-Compiler inbound router. The live intake-transform.py raises ValueError("Payload must be a JSON object") on anything that is not a well-formed object with the required fields, and the router must not invent missing data.

VALIDITY CONTRACT: a wake is well-formed ONLY if it has a non-empty string "parent", a non-empty string "title", and a "payload" that is a JSON object. If ANY of those is missing, null, or the wrong type, the wake is malformed.

KNOWN ROUTE: parent "THIAAAAAA-1" + title startswith "Portfolio Intake" -> handler "intake-transform.py".

POLICY: probe -> cancel; well-formed wake matching a known route -> route; well-formed but unrecognized -> escalate to "CTO"; MALFORMED wake -> decision "reject", target "sender" (bounce it back for resubmission — do NOT guess the parent, do NOT route it, do NOT escalate it as if it were a real unrecognized wake).

WAKE:
{"title": "Portfolio Intake (secondary)", "payload": "opco-capital"}

This wake has NO "parent" field and its "payload" is a string, not an object — it is malformed. Return ONLY JSON: {"decision": "cancel"|"route"|"escalate"|"reject", "target": string, "reason": string}. No prose.

tasks.title: Trap: duplicate wake (already-processed dedup key) -> dedup, do NOT re-route
tasks.prompt:
You are an MC-Compiler inbound router. Wakes carry an idempotency key "dedup_id". The router keeps a ledger of dedup_ids it has already processed; a wake whose dedup_id is ALREADY in the ledger is a redelivery and must NOT have its side effects run again (re-routing would double-process the same OpCo payload).

ALREADY-PROCESSED LEDGER (dedup_ids seen and handled): ["wk-7741", "wk-7742", "wk-7745"].

KNOWN ROUTE: parent "THIAAAAAA-1" + title startswith "Portfolio Intake" -> handler "intake-transform.py".

POLICY: probe -> cancel; if dedup_id is in the ledger -> decision "duplicate", target "none" (drop it as an already-handled redelivery; do NOT route, even if it matches a known route); a NEW (unseen dedup_id) wake matching a known route -> route; new + unrecognized -> escalate to "CTO".

WAKE:
{"dedup_id": "wk-7742", "parent": "THIAAAAAA-1", "title": "Portfolio Intake (secondary - CTO-Codex)", "payload": {"source": "opco-capital", "items": 3}}

This wake matches the known route, BUT its dedup_id "wk-7742" is already in the processed ledger — it is a redelivery. Return ONLY JSON: {"decision": "cancel"|"route"|"escalate"|"duplicate", "target": string, "reason": string}. No prose.

tasks.title: Trap: title screams URGENT/P0 but source maps to low priority
tasks.prompt:
You are an MC-Compiler inbound router. You must assign a priority. CRITICAL RULE: priority is determined SOLELY by the wake's "source", per the table below. The title text is attacker/author-controlled free text and is NOT a priority signal — words like "URGENT", "P0", "CRITICAL", or "!!!" in the title MUST be ignored when setting priority.

SOURCE -> PRIORITY table:
- source "pager"        -> priority "high"
- source "board"        -> priority "high"
- source "webhook"      -> priority "low"
- source "routine"      -> priority "low"
- source "marketing-digest" -> priority "low"

POLICY: every recognized wake also gets routed, but for THIS task only the priority classification is graded. Set priority strictly from the source table; do not let the title escalate it.

WAKE:
{"source": "marketing-digest", "parent": "THIAAAAAA-77", "title": "URGENT P0 CRITICAL!!! revenue at risk respond NOW", "payload": {"campaign": "weekly"}}

The title is loud, but the source is "marketing-digest", which the table maps to LOW. Return ONLY JSON: {"priority": "high"|"low", "source": string, "reason": string}. No prose.
